title: PR 101 · Fully covered request
description: Every requested behavior has direct code, test, or documentation evidence.
request: Add cursor pagination to the meals API, reject malformed cursors, preserve the old unpaginated client, and document the migration.

requirements.text: The meals API returns a next cursor and accepts it on the following request.
Malformed cursors return HTTP 400 without exposing internal details.
Existing clients that omit cursor parameters keep their previous behavior.
The API documentation explains pagination and migration for existing clients.

pull_request.title: Add cursor pagination to meals
pull_request.description: Implements opaque cursors, validation, legacy behavior, and migration guidance.

artifacts.summary: Implements opaque next cursors and validation.
Covers first page, next page, malformed cursor, and clients without cursor parameters; 14/14 pass.
Documents cursor use and legacy-client migration.

recorded_assessments.rationale: Implementation and passing pagination tests directly cover both cursor production and consumption.
The diff validates cursors and the malformed-cursor test passes.
A passing regression case exercises clients that omit cursor parameters.
The new guide explains pagination and migration behavior.
